title: attp Token
description: Agent-to-Agent Transfer Protocol token format. A self-describing, versioned JSON envelope for cross-agent, cross-machine context transfer.

attp.description: Protocol version as a major.minor string. Consumers MUST reject tokens with an unrecognized major version. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields from newer minor versions.

id.description: Globally unique token identifier. Format: attp_ prefix followed by 20+ base62 characters.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone indicating when the token was assembled.

payloads.description: Content items included in the token, each either inlined or referenced for lazy fetch. May be empty.

requests.description: Structured work requests from sender to receiver.

decisions.description: Decisions made by the sender that the receiver should be aware of.

extensions.description: Namespaced extension data. Keys use reverse-domain naming (e.g., 'demarch.interweave'). Consumers MUST ignore unknown extensions.

provenance.description: Origin and chain-of-custody metadata for the token.

participants.description: Map of agent_id to participant metadata. Keys are agent identifiers that correspond to vector_clock entries.

vector_clock.description: Logical clock mapping agent_id to an integer counter. Enables causal ordering of tokens across multiple agents without synchronized wall clocks.

chain.description: Previous tokens in the conversation chain, in chronological order. Each entry is a summary, not the full token. Append-only; producers MAY truncate to the last 20 entries for long conversations.

sequence.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number within a conversation. First token is 1. Enables ordering even if timestamps are clock-skewed.

origin.description: Identity of the agent that produced this token.

session_id.description: Opaque session identifier from the agent runtime. Optional because some agents do not expose session IDs.

hostname.description: Machine hostname. Always present as a human-readable identifier.

tailscale_id.description: Tailscale machine identity. Present when transport is Tailscale.

participant.description: Metadata about a participant in the token exchange.

vector_clock_index.description: The participant's current position in the vector clock at the time they last touched the token.

chain_entry.description: Summary of a previous token in the conversation chain.

agent_id.description: Agent that produced the previous token.

machine.description: Machine where the previous token was produced.

timestamp.description: When the previous token was produced.

repo.description: Repository state snapshot at the time the token was created.

url.description: Canonical repo URL. Used for identity, not cloning.

commit.description: Full commit SHA at token creation time.

dirty_paths.description: Repo-relative paths with uncommitted changes. May be empty.

merkle_root.description: Content-addressed root hash of the repo tree. Format: algorithm:hex (e.g., 'sha256:abcdef...').

sensitivity.description: Exclusion manifest and cryptographic attestation. Declares what was left out of the token and why.

has_exclusions.description: Whether any paths were excluded from the token. Quick check before inspecting excluded_paths.

excluded_paths.description: Repo-relative paths that were excluded. Directories end with '/'. Empty array when has_exclusions is false.

exclusion_attestation.description: Cryptographic proof that the listed paths were actually excluded from the token's content.

merkle_root.description: Root hash of the Merkle tree covering the full repo tree (including excluded paths). Format: algorithm:hex.

nonce.description: Random nonce to prevent replay of attestations across tokens.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the attestation fields. Verification method depends on transport (e.g., Tailscale node key).

payload.description: A content item, discriminated by the mode field. Inline payloads carry content directly; ref payloads point to content for lazy fetch.

role.description: Semantic role of the payload (e.g., 'source', 'config', 'test', 'doc'). Optional hint for consumers.

content_type.description: MIME type or text/x-{lang} for source files.

hash.description: Content hash in algorithm:hex format. Consumers SHOULD verify hash matches content.

role.description: Semantic role of the payload (e.g., 'source', 'config', 'test', 'doc'). Optional hint for consumers.

fetch_via.description: Fetch descriptor for lazy content retrieval.

content_hash.description: Content hash in algorithm:hex format. Used as cache key and integrity check after fetch.

size_bytes.description: Content size in bytes. Allows the consumer to decide whether to fetch before committing.

tool.description: MCP tool name to call for lazy fetch (e.g., 'attp_fetch_file'). Defaults to 'attp_fetch_file' in v1.

content_type.description: MIME type or text/x-{lang} for source files.

hash.description: Content hash in algorithm:hex format (same as fetch_via.content_hash, duplicated at top level for uniform access).

size_bytes.description: Content size in bytes (same as fetch_via.size_bytes, duplicated at top level for uniform access).

request.description: A structured work request from sender to receiver.

summary.description: Human-readable description of what is being requested.

params.description: Request-specific parameters. Schema varies by kind.

decision.description: A decision made by the sender that the receiver should know about.

decided_at.description: When the decision was made, in ISO 8601 format.
